name: Lobster Lairs
region: Asia, Malaysia, Borneo Island, Sabah, Sipadan Island
description: This site off Sipadan Island features dramatic underwater cliffs plunging over 600 meters. Schools of barracuda and jacks patrol the blue while turtles glide past. The reef wall is dotted with crevices sheltering lobsters and colorful reef fish. Strong currents bring pelagics, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. The steep drop-off creates stunning underwater vistas.

name: South Point
region: Asia, Malaysia, Borneo Island, Sabah, Sipadan Island
description: Drift along steep walls covered in vibrant corals as barracuda and jacks patrol the blue. Turtles glide past while schools of bumphead parrotfish graze below. Strong currents attract pelagics, making this a thrilling dive. The wall drops into the abyss, offering dramatic topography. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. Expect encounters with reef sharks and occasional hammerheads. Best for experienced divers due to currents.

name: Lost World
region: Asia, Malaysia, Borneo Island, Sabah, Sipadan Kapalai
description: A stunning wall dive plunging into the deep blue, teeming with barracuda, jacks, and reef sharks. Vibrant coral gardens cover the shallows, while turtles and schools of trevallies cruise the current. Dramatic drop-offs and overhangs create thrilling swim-throughs. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, with strong currents attracting pelagics. Part of a protected marine park, this site offers pristine conditions and unforgettable encounters.

name: Twin Reef
region: Asia, Malaysia, Borneo Island, Sabah, Sipadan Kapalai
description: This site features two stunning coral reefs separated by a sandy channel, teeming with marine life. Expect schools of barracuda, bumphead parrotfish, and turtles gliding through clear waters. The vibrant hard and soft corals attract colorful reef fish, while occasional white tip reef sharks patrol the edges. Depths range from 5 to 30 meters, suitable for all levels. Strong currents may occur, bringing pelagics. The dramatic drop-offs and overhangs create exciting swim-throughs. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. Located in the biodiverse Celebes Sea, this spot offers world-class diving.

name: Blue Liberty Wreck
region: Asia, Malaysia, Borneo Island, Sabah, Sipadan Kapalai
description: An intact WWII wreck resting at 30 meters, covered in vibrant corals and swarming with schooling barracuda, trevally, and batfish. Strong currents attract pelagics, making it ideal for drift diving. The wreck's structure offers swim-throughs and overhangs teeming with macro life. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. Advanced divers will love exploring its history and marine biodiversity. Located near Sipadan Kapalai, where the Celebes Sea's nutrient-rich waters create unforgettable encounters.

name: Kapalai Rock
region: Asia, Malaysia, Borneo Island, Sabah, Sipadan Kapalai
description: This stunning site features a submerged rock formation surrounded by vibrant coral gardens in the Celebes Sea. Expect excellent visibility and gentle currents, perfect for spotting schools of barracuda, reef sharks, and turtles. The sloping reef drops to sandy patches where garden eels and stingrays hide. Colorful soft corals and sea fans attract macro life like nudibranchs and pygmy seahorses. Ideal for both beginners and experienced divers, with warm waters year-round.
